issues.title: The Great Acceleration
issues: Opens with hyperbolic framing: 'We are living through the most consequential six-year window in human history' — unsubstantiated claim presented as fact
Promotional closing: 'The acceleration is real. We are preparing for it.' reads like manifesto, not research
Synthetic volatility indices (6.2, 8.4, 9.1, 9.7) with no methodology shown for how these composites were calculated
'capability overhang', 'coordination collapse' presented as high-probability failure modes without evidence
suggestedFixes: Qualify the opening claim: 'potentially one of the most consequential' or frame as hypothesis
Remove manifesto-style closing; end with specific next steps or data preview
Add methodology note for volatility index calculation or label as illustrative estimates
Ground failure mode probabilities with historical analogies or cited expert analysis

issues.title: Zen and the Art of Vibe Coding
issues: Uses 'leverage' four times in context of productivity ('infinite leverage', 'high leverage') — reads as self-help/business buzzword territory
Unsubstantiated health claim: 'It's probably why presidents and CEOs age so visibly. Look at Obama before and after office' — causal assertion without evidence, also politically loaded
'This is the curve that gets the most attention, and it is the easiest to measure. But it is not the most dangerous' — dramatic framing without backing
Missing source attribution for any of the psychological claims about leverage stress
suggestedFixes: Replace 'infinite leverage' with specific description of what that means in practical terms
Remove the Obama/Trump comparison or replace with cited research on executive stress
Add at least one cited source for the psychological effects of high-productivity environments
Ground 'not the most dangerous' with a specific example or data point

issues.title: Public APIs Usefulness Review: 400 APIs, 5 Tiers, 10 That Matter
issues: Meta footer reads like editorial notes leaked into the article: 'Article: 309 lines, 13,086 bytes' — should be in git metadata, not visible content
suggestedFixes: Remove the article self-referential line count and byte size from the public-facing methodology note

summary: Reviewed 15 articles. 12 of 15 are clean — well-structured, evidence-grounded, and appropriately cautious. These read like research lab notes with specific observations, explicit uncertainty markers, and data tables. No marketing language issues. Two articles have medium-severity problems: 'The Great Acceleration' uses hyperbolic framing and unsubstantiated claims that read more like a manifesto than lab findings. 'Zen and the Art of Vibe Coding' mixes genuine operational insight with buzzy 'leverage' language and an unsupported political comparison. One minor issue in the public APIs article (metadata in visible content). The remaining articles — OpenRAG deployment, API provider framework, model selection framework, Just Rebuild It, character consistency, image models, RAG without hallucination, nanoclaw vs openclaw, codex53 vs opus46, aesthetic scoring, tutor architecture, and image-model benchmark — all maintain strong lab tone with specific observations, honest uncertainty acknowledgments, and appropriate use of synthetic data labels. No game-changing, revolutionary, seamless, robust, transformative, unleash, supercharge, empower, unlock, harness, or dive deep found anywhere.
